title: Crowded Corners
intro: A ‘diversified’ fund shelf can still concentrate risk. Sort each signal: true diversification vs crowded overlap.

buckets.label: Crowded / overlap
buckets.hint: Same bet dressed as many tickets

cards.text: Three ‘diversified’ equity funds that share the same top-10 names
Mixing large-cap, mid-cap, and international equity with low name overlap
Sector funds in IT + a ‘flexi’ that is 40% the same IT leaders
Debt sleeve for emergencies separate from long equity SIPs
Four thematic funds that all lean on the same macro narrative
Index fund + a tightly constrained satelllite you sized intentionally small

round2.text: High active share fund whose active bets are the same five crowded midcaps everyone owns
Comparing portfolio holding lists before buying another ‘similar’ category fund
Owning the same bank stock via stock + banking fund + ‘flexi’ heavy finance

debrief.summary: Diversification is about what drives returns and risk together — not the number of fund folios. Overlap and crowded trades can hide inside tidy category labels.
debrief.principle: Count shared exposures, not just product count.
